title: pterilis
intro: Pterilis is another name for the plant Pteris multifida, commonly known as the toothed fern or lace fern. This plant is a type of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves of the pterilis plant have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of pterilis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in combination with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, or salves.
